title: Reviewer Worktree from PR Branch + Dirty Check
summary: After a pr_opened event, create a fresh worktree for the reviewer from the pushed PR branch (origin/golemic/issue-<N>), configure git identity for the reviewer bot, write a worktree_created event with role: reviewer, and after the reviewer run, perform a git status --porcelain dirty check that maps to review_failed on dirty state.

stakeholder_intent.goal: Create an isolated worktree for the reviewer that shows exactly the pushed PR branch content, and verify the reviewer did not modify it
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Ensures the reviewer sees exactly the code that would be merged (not dev worktree artifacts), and enforces read-only convention via a deterministic dirty check after the run
stakeholder_intent.trigger: After the dev role finishes and a pr_opened event exists in the log, the runner calls worktree.Create for the reviewer role
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: A fresh worktree from origin/golemic/issue-<N> is created, worktree_created event (role: reviewer) is written, and after the reviewer run, git status --porcelain confirms no files were modified

in_scope: git fetch origin + git worktree add from origin/golemic/issue-<N> (detached or tracking branch)
Local git config: same env-based credential helper as I1.4, but with reviewer bot identity
After reviewer run: git status --porcelain check → dirty → review_failed outcome
out_of_scope: Loop orchestration beyond the dirty check mapping

preconditions: pr_opened event exists in the event log (dev has pushed branch and opened PR)
Event log reader (I1.1) is available to read pr_opened event

business_rules.rule: The reviewer worktree is created from the remote PR branch (origin/golemic/issue-<N>), not from the dev worktree
business_rules.outcome: git worktree add <path> origin/golemic/issue-<N> (detached HEAD, no new branch)

business_rules.rule: The reviewer git identity is the reviewer bot login (not the dev bot)
business_rules.applies_when: Configuring the reviewer worktree's local git config
business_rules.outcome: user.name and user.email are set to the reviewer bot identity

business_rules.rule: After the reviewer run, git status --porcelain is run in the reviewer worktree
business_rules.outcome: If git status --porcelain output is non-empty, the outcome is review_failed

business_rules.rule: If git status --porcelain is empty (clean), the run proceeds normally
business_rules.outcome: No special action; loop continues to evaluate review_submitted event

business_rules.rule: The reviewer worktree is removed on success outcome (same as dev worktree)

then.result: Worktree created, review proceeds, dirty check passes

interfaces.name: Reviewer Worktree Creator
interfaces.operation: CreateForReviewer(repoRoot, golemicDir, runId, issueNumber, branchName, reviewerBotLogin, executor, eventWriter) error

inputs.name: reviewerBotLogin
constraints: Must be the GitHub login of the reviewer bot
inputs.source: gh api user with reviewer token

outputs.body: worktree_created event with role: reviewer written to log

errors.message: Remote branch origin/golemic/issue-<N> not found; was the branch pushed?

integration_contracts.operation: git fetch origin + git worktree add <path> origin/golemic/issue-<N>
integration_contracts.request_contract: git fetch origin then git worktree add <path> origin/golemic/issue-<N>
integration_contracts.idempotency: Worktree add is not idempotent; fails if path already exists
integration_contracts.failure_mapping: Non-zero exit → REMOTE_BRANCH_NOT_FOUND or generic error

integration_contracts.transport: git status --porcelain (run in reviewer worktree cwd)
integration_contracts.operation: Check if worktree has uncommitted changes
integration_contracts.response_contract: Empty string if clean; non-empty if dirty

changes.value_source: worktree_created event with path, branch, baseSha, role: reviewer

security.authentication: Reviewer token (GH_TOKEN) is used for git fetch and git config; inherited from parent process
authorization_rules: The reviewer token must have read access to the host repo
security.data_classification: Repository data (the reviewer sees the full PR branch content)
audit_requirements: worktree_created event records the reviewer worktree creation

architecture_constraints: Must share the internal/worktree package with I1.4 (same git operations, different branch source and role)
Worktree source is origin/golemic/issue-<N> (remote branch), not origin/main
Dirty check runs after the reviewer agent process exits
allowed_changes: Add CreateForReviewer and IsDirty functions to internal/worktree
forbidden_changes: Do not create reviewer worktree from the dev worktree path
Do not skip the dirty check
implementation_context.migration_strategy: Extension of existing internal/worktree package; no migration needed
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Single-threaded per run; reviewer worktree creation follows dev completion

acceptance_scenarios.title: Reviewer worktree is created from remote branch
given: A fake executor that records git commands
then: git worktree add uses origin/golemic/issue-42 as the source (not origin/main)

acceptance_scenarios.title: Dirty check passes on clean worktree
given: A fake executor that returns empty string for git status --porcelain

acceptance_scenarios.title: Dirty check triggers review_failed
given: A fake executor that returns 'M file.go' for git status --porcelain
then: Outcome review_failed is triggered by the loop orchestrator (I1.10)

acceptance_scenarios.title: Reviewer git identity uses reviewer bot login
given: A fake executor that records git config commands
then: git config user.name and user.email are set to 'reviewer-bot'
The identity differs from the dev bot identity

acceptance_scenarios.title: Reviewer worktree is removed on success
given: A worktree exists for the reviewer
when: worktree.Cleanup is called for the reviewer worktree
then: git worktree remove is called for the reviewer worktree path

test_cases: Reviewer worktree source is the remote PR branch
Dirty check returns false for clean worktree
Dirty check returns true for dirty worktree
Dirty check failure maps to review_failed
Reviewer git identity uses reviewer bot login
non_functional_requirements: All git commands go through the injectable Executor interface
definition_of_done: go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test -count=1 ./... is green
Reviewer worktree is created from remote PR branch (not dev worktree)
Dirty check is implemented and maps to review_failed

decision_log.decision: Create reviewer worktree from origin/golemic/issue-<N> (remote PR branch, detached HEAD)
decision_log.rationale: Specified in backlog: 'frischer Worktree ... von origin/golemic/issue-<N> (detached oder Tracking-Branch)' and §2.10: 'Der Reviewer arbeitet nicht im Dev-Worktree, sondern in einem frischen Worktree vom gepushten PR-Branch'

decision_log.decision: Use git status --porcelain after the reviewer run; non-empty output → review_failed
decision_log.rationale: Specified in backlog: 'git status --porcelain — dirty → Fehlerpfad review_failed'

decision_log.decision: Reviewer worktree creation uses the same internal/worktree package as dev worktree (I1.4)
decision_log.rationale: Both create git worktrees with git config; only the branch source and role differ

decision_log.decision: Reviewer worktree uses the same Executor interface as dev worktree
decision_log.rationale: The Executor interface in internal/preflight is used for all git commands in both dev and reviewer worktree operations

codebase_evidence.finding: Executor interface with Run and RunWithEnv methods exists; reviewer worktree commands will use this interface
